title: erigeron glabratus
intro: Erigeron glabratus, also known as rough fleabane, is a perennial plant species native to eastern North America, characterized by its white or pink flowers and coarse leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. Erigeron glabratus is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and acting as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often dried and used to make teas or infusions, which can be used to treat various ailments.
